people.text: Paula wants a hands-on art workshop where she can make something to take home. She is free on Sunday and prefers not to spend more than two hours there. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

Ryan is interested in space and wants a workshop with real scientists. He needs activities suitable for adults, not only children. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

Mei wants to take her eight-year-old brother to a museum activity. He likes dinosaurs, but he gets bored if there is too much reading. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

Leo wants to learn about local history through old photographs. He needs wheelchair access and would like a small group where he can ask questions. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

Grace wants a creative digital workshop for teenagers. She would like to use tablets or computers and prefers an activity connected with animation or games. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

texts.text: Print and Paint runs on Sundays from 2 p.m. to 3:30 p.m. Participants design simple patterns, print them on fabric bags and take their work home. The workshop is friendly, practical and suitable for beginners who enjoy making art. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions.

Meet the Astronomers is an evening workshop for adults at the Science Museum. Researchers explain current space projects and help visitors use computer simulations. There is time for questions, and the session is not designed for young children. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

Dino Dig is a lively workshop for children aged six to ten. Young visitors handle model bones, search for fossils in sand trays and build a dinosaur skeleton together. Instructions are mostly spoken, with very little reading required. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

Local Lives uses historic photographs to explore how the town has changed. Groups are limited to ten people, and the guide encourages discussion. The room is fully wheelchair accessible and has large screens for viewing images clearly. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

Game Frames is a digital workshop for ages thirteen to sixteen. Participants use tablets to create short animations and simple game characters. Museum staff introduce basic design ideas, and no previous coding experience is needed. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

Space Kids Club introduces planets through songs, colouring and simple games. It is designed for children under seven, and adults mainly watch. There are no scientists present, though museum educators lead the activities enthusiastically. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

Photo Archive Open Day lets visitors look through old local photographs independently. The archive room has stairs and no lift. Staff can answer basic questions, but there is no guided workshop or group discussion. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

Clay Studio lasts three hours on Saturday mornings. Participants make a small bowl and collect it a week later after firing. It is practical and creative, but nothing can be taken home on the day. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

answers.explanation: Paula wants a Sunday hands-on art activity under two hours with something to take home; A fits.
Ryan needs adult space activities with scientists; B matches.
Mei needs a dinosaur activity for an eight-year-old with little reading; C fits.
Leo needs local history photos, access and a small discussion group; D matches.
Grace wants a teen digital animation or game workshop; E fits.
